beats.title: Council Aftermath
beats.boundaryReason: The session opens immediately after the council meeting as the party recaps the evacuation plan, reviews the map, and starts deciding what errands to handle before leaving town.
dateEvidence: The DM says the party has just stepped out of the council meeting.
The manifest starts the session on 1748-04-04.
The DM says they have the afternoon before the Festival of Rebirth begins the next evening.

beats.title: Errands in Karawa
beats.boundaryReason: The party splits up around Karawa, gathering local information from the Charmhearts, inspecting the mesa and temple, and questioning Candrosa about the north.
dateEvidence: This beat continues directly from the afternoon council aftermath.
The scenes remain focused on same-day errands before Alesh's evening departure.

beats.title: Supplies and Farewell
beats.boundaryReason: Preparation shifts into logistics as the group buys supplies, Seeker plans temple fortifications, and Alesh rides out south after agreeing on a rendezvous plan.
dateEvidence: The transcript stays on the same pre-festival day until Alesh says they are leaving that evening before the festival.
The beat ends when Alesh rides off and the DM asks what the party does before tomorrow's festival.

beats.title: Fortify and Meet Riswynn
beats.boundaryReason: A new day begins with Seeker reinforcing the temple, the party finishing preparations, and Riswynn arriving at the caravanserai to join the Raven's Hold expedition.
dateEvidence: The DM asks what the party does before tomorrow's festival, then moves into work that takes most of the next day.
The beat ends with Riswynn explaining her background shortly before the festival begins.
The manifest covers the next day of preparations before the evening festival rite.

beats.title: Festival Ambush Begins
beats.boundaryReason: The first Jeevali rite and the next day's procession frame the town's fear until the ambush erupts at the edge of the crowd and the opening rounds of combat begin.
dateEvidence: The DM narrates the first night of the festival, then explicitly says that in the morning the procession continues.
The ambush begins during the next afternoon's shrine procession, so this beat spans two consecutive festival days.

beats.title: Maws in the Crowd
beats.boundaryReason: The fight tightens as the maw-creatures crash into the party's line, everyone scrambles for position, and Kenzo takes the brunt of the assault.
dateEvidence: This beat begins in the middle of the ambush after the procession scene has already shifted into combat.
The battle is still happening in the same afternoon festival crowd outside town.

beats.title: Gnolls Broken
beats.boundaryReason: The tide turns as the remaining attackers are cut down, one gnoll is left sleeping and bound, and the immediate combat gives way to emergency triage and decisions.
dateEvidence: The action continues without a scene break from the earlier combat phase.
The beat ends once initiative effectively stops and the party starts discussing what to do with the sleeping gnoll.

beats.title: Flight at First Light
beats.boundaryReason: After the battle the party examines the surviving gnoll and strange maws, confers with Havdar and Candrosa, then spends a tense night preparing while the town flees west at dawn.
dateEvidence: The beat starts immediately after initiative ends on the day of the ambush.
The transcript then explicitly moves through the evening and into the next morning's evacuation and departure.
The morning departure is inferred to be the following day because the party rests overnight before the dawn exodus.
